framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: Every AI use case must have clear owners with defined accountability for AI governance across the organisation.
mandatory_records: AI governance policy|job descriptions|executive accountable official assignment|responsible-parties register|governance forum minutes
notes: Guidance names two roles: Executive accountable official (org-wide); Responsible parties (approve/operate/monitor each AI system)

framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: Identify affected groups and potential harms before deployment; plan mitigation accordingly.
mandatory_records: AI screening/intake form|impact assessment record|stakeholder-identification record
notes: Pre-deployment; covers end-users third parties public and environment

framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: Treat AI use like any other material business risk — identify score treat and monitor.
mandatory_records: enterprise risk register entries|risk treatment records|control records|risk review schedule
notes: Integrate with existing ERM; do not create a parallel AI-only risk process

framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: Maintain transparency about AI use and limitations — internally to staff and externally to end-users and stakeholders.
mandatory_records: AI system register|privacy notices|supplier AI disclosure|end-user disclosure content|model-card equivalent
notes: AI system register is the keystone artefact; external disclosure required for consequential AI

framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: AI systems must be tested before use and monitored in life; incidents captured and actioned against pre-agreed thresholds.
mandatory_records: pre-deployment test results|monitoring metrics|drift-detection records|incident thresholds|incident log
notes: Incident thresholds must be defined up front not post-hoc

framework_name: Guidance for AI Adoption — 6 Essential Practices
framework_publisher: Australian Government Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: People remain responsible for decisions and outcomes — AI supports but does not replace human judgement on consequential actions.
mandatory_records: human-oversight process documentation|training records|appeals/recourse procedures|override log
notes: Approval override and appeals pathways must be documented per AI use case

control_description: Policies processes procedures and practices across the organisation related to the mapping measuring and managing of AI risks are in place transparent and implemented effectively.
mandatory_records: AI risk policy|process documentation|implementation evidence

control_description: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered responsible and trained for mapping measuring and managing AI risks.
mandatory_records: accountability structure chart|training records|role assignments

control_name: Workforce diversity equity inclusion and accessibility
control_description: Workforce diversity equity inclusion and accessibility processes are prioritised in the mapping measuring and managing of AI risks throughout the lifecycle.
mandatory_records: DEIA policy|workforce composition record|accessibility review
notes: DEIA grouped under accountability family as workforce accountability

control_name: Commitment to AI risk management culture
control_description: Organisational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.
mandatory_records: culture assessment|risk-awareness training records
notes: Risk culture is a dimension of organisational accountability

control_description: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.
mandatory_records: stakeholder engagement log|feedback integration record

control_description: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.
mandatory_records: supplier AI assessment|supply-chain risk register|contracts

control_description: Context is established and understood.
mandatory_records: context document|operating envelope|use case description

control_description: Categorisation of the AI system is performed.
mandatory_records: system categorisation record|risk-tier assignment

control_description: AI capabilities targeted usage goals and expected benefits and costs are understood.
mandatory_records: capability statement|cost-benefit analysis

control_description: Risks and benefits are mapped for all components of the AI system including third-party software and data.
mandatory_records: third-party component inventory|risk assessment

control_name: Impacts to individuals groups and society
control_description: Impacts to individuals groups communities organisations and society are characterised.
mandatory_records: impact characterisation|affected-group analysis

control_description: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.
mandatory_records: metrics catalog|measurement methodology

control_description: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics (validity reliability safety security privacy transparency accountability fairness).
mandatory_records: evaluation results|trustworthiness scorecard

control_description: Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time are in place.
mandatory_records: risk tracking log|trend analysis

control_description: Feedback about efficacy of measurement is gathered and assessed.
mandatory_records: feedback log|measurement-improvement actions

control_description: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from Map and Measure are prioritised responded to and managed.
mandatory_records: prioritised risk list|response decisions

control_description: Strategies to maximise AI benefits and minimise negative impacts are planned prepared implemented documented and informed by input from relevant AI actors.
mandatory_records: benefit-maximisation plan|stakeholder input

control_description: AI risks and benefits from third-party resources are regularly monitored and risk controls are applied and documented.
mandatory_records: third-party monitoring log|control evidence

control_description: Risk treatments including response and recovery and communication plans for the identified and measured AI risks are documented and monitored regularly.
mandatory_records: risk treatment plan|incident response playbook|communication plan

control_description: AI systems must operate safely securely and robustly with risks identified assessed and managed throughout the AI life cycle.
mandatory_records: safety testing records|security controls|robustness test evidence|incident response plan
notes: Cross-sectoral; interpreted by each UK sector regulator within their remit

control_description: Parties directly affected by AI should be able to access sufficient information about AI systems to scrutinise and challenge outcomes.
mandatory_records: transparency notices|explainability documentation|model cards or equivalents
notes: Proportional to risk and context not full explainability for every system

control_description: AI systems should not undermine legal rights or discriminate unfairly or create unfair commercial outcomes.
mandatory_records: bias testing evidence|fairness metrics|remediation log
notes: Fairness as impact-on-groups; also touches risk_management (bias risk) via bridge relationship

control_description: Governance measures should ensure effective oversight of AI supply and use with clear accountability for outcomes.
mandatory_records: accountability assignments|governance committee records|supply-chain contracts

control_description: Users and affected third parties should be able to contest AI decisions and seek redress where outcomes are harmful or unfair.
mandatory_records: complaints process|appeals log|redress outcomes
notes: Especially salient for consumer-facing and high-impact AI

framework_name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A — AI Management System controls
control_description: Organisation-wide AI policies establishing intent scope and responsibilities.
mandatory_records: AI policy|supporting procedures
notes: Expand to individual controls when ISO/IEC 42001:2023 acquired (paywalled)

framework_name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A — AI Management System controls
control_description: AI-specific roles responsibilities and reporting lines within the organisation.
mandatory_records: role assignments|responsibility matrix|reporting lines
notes: Expand

framework_name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A — AI Management System controls
control_description: Ensuring adequate human data and computational resources for AI systems.
mandatory_records: resource allocation plan|capacity records
notes: Org-level resource allocation grouped under accountability family

framework_name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A — AI Management System controls
control_description: Impact assessments on individuals groups and society.
mandatory_records: impact assessment reports|affected-group analysis
notes: Expand

framework_name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A — AI Management System controls
control_description: Controls across design development deployment and retirement phases.
mandatory_records: lifecycle documentation|phase gate records
notes: Lifecycle includes pre-deployment test + ongoing monitoring + retirement

framework_name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A — AI Management System controls
control_description: Data governance including provenance quality and handling.
mandatory_records: data catalog|provenance records|quality reports
notes: Expand

framework_name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A — AI Management System controls
control_name: Information for interested parties of AI systems
control_description: Transparency and disclosure to users stakeholders and regulators.
mandatory_records: disclosure materials|user notices
notes: Expand

framework_name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A — AI Management System controls
control_description: Appropriate use including human oversight.
mandatory_records: use policies|oversight records
notes: Expand

framework_name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A — AI Management System controls
control_description: Managing AI-related risks in third-party and customer interactions.
mandatory_records: third-party agreements|customer communication records
notes: Expand

control_description: Developer of a high-risk AI system must use reasonable care to protect consumers from known or reasonably foreseeable risks of algorithmic discrimination.
mandatory_records: developer documentation|risk mitigation evidence|developer-to-deployer disclosure
notes: Verify exact obligations against enacted text of SB24-205

control_description: Deployer of a high-risk AI system must implement a risk management policy and program that is iterative and reviewed at least annually.
mandatory_records: risk management policy|program implementation records|annual review evidence
notes: Expand

control_description: Deployer must complete an impact assessment before deployment and annually thereafter for each high-risk AI system.
mandatory_records: annual impact assessment report|pre-deployment impact assessment
notes: Expand

control_description: Pre-decision notice to consumers subject to consequential decisions made or substantially assisted by high-risk AI.
mandatory_records: consumer notice content|delivery records
notes: Expand

control_name: Right to correct data and appeal
control_description: Consumers must be able to correct inaccurate personal data and appeal adverse consequential decisions.
mandatory_records: appeal process documentation|appeals log|data correction log
notes: Expand

control_description: Algorithmic discrimination incidents reported to the Colorado Attorney General within the statutory window.
mandatory_records: AG notification records|incident-to-report mapping
notes: Verify statutory notification window against enacted text

control_description: Written supervisory procedures must address AI use in securities activities including roles responsibilities escalation and exception handling.
mandatory_records: WSPs addressing AI|supervisory review records|exception log
notes: Supervision = human oversight of AI use in securities context

control_description: Pre-deployment testing and ongoing validation of AI systems affecting customer activity trading or recommendations.
mandatory_records: test plans|validation results|ongoing monitoring reports
notes: Expand

control_description: Preserve records of AI-generated communications and recommendations consistent with SEA Rules 17a-3 and 17a-4 as applicable.
mandatory_records: AI-output records|retention schedule|recordkeeping mapping
notes: Reference Rules 17a-3 and 17a-4 precisely when verifying

control_description: AI-generated communications with the public must meet FINRA Rule 2210 content standards and principal approval requirements.
mandatory_records: AI communication review log|disclosure templates|principal approvals
notes: Customer comms = disclosure/transparency family

control_description: Third-party AI vendor risk managed consistent with Regulation S-P and FINRA cybersecurity guidance.
mandatory_records: vendor risk assessments|cyber controls|Reg S-P evidence
notes: Vendor cyber grouped under third_party family; standalone cyber family can be added later if volume justifies

framework_name: FCA DP5/22 Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning + 2024 AI Update
control_description: Senior Management Function holders are accountable for AI systems used in UK regulated activities under SM&CR.
mandatory_records: SMF statements of responsibility covering AI|allocation records|MRT mapping
notes: Verify exact SMF alignment post-2024 AI Update

framework_name: FCA DP5/22 Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning + 2024 AI Update
control_description: Model risk management principles applied to AI/ML proportionate to firm type (SS1/23 for PRA-regulated banks; principles-based for FCA-only firms).
mandatory_records: model inventory|model risk assessments|validation records
notes: SS1/23 applies to PRA banks; FCA firms proportionate

framework_name: FCA DP5/22 Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning + 2024 AI Update
control_description: AI-mediated customer outcomes must meet the Consumer Duty cross-cutting rules and retail customer outcomes framework.
mandatory_records: AI-to-customer-outcome mapping|monitoring evidence|Consumer Duty board report
notes: Consumer Duty is about impact on customer outcomes

framework_name: FCA DP5/22 Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning + 2024 AI Update
control_description: AI systems must fit within firms' operational resilience framework and important business services mapping including impact tolerances.
mandatory_records: IBS mapping including AI|impact tolerance statements|severe but plausible scenarios
notes: Op resilience as sub-domain of risk_management; standalone family addable later if volume justifies

framework_name: FCA DP5/22 Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning + 2024 AI Update
control_description: Outsourcing and third-party risk rules apply to AI vendors and model providers; DORA alignment where relevant for cross-border firms.
mandatory_records: vendor inventory including AI|outsourcing assessments|DORA-equivalent controls
notes: Update for DORA alignment where relevant for UK-EU cross-border

control_description: Entities must include AI systems in their operational risk management framework; AI is treated as a significant operational and governance risk by APRA.
mandatory_records: operational risk profile|AI inventory|AI risk heat map|board-reviewed risk appetite statement
notes: Verify exact CPS 230 final text — stub authored from APRA AI engagement summary Oct 2025

control_description: Entities must manage risks arising from third-party AI providers as part of service provider management obligations under CPS 230.
mandatory_records: third-party AI inventory|provider risk assessments|service-level agreements|exit/continuity plan
notes: CPS 230 explicitly covers material service providers including cloud LLM vendors

control_description: Where AI supports critical operations the entity must maintain business continuity and recovery capability consistent with CPS 230 tolerance statements.
mandatory_records: critical operations map including AI|BCP for AI-dependent processes|tolerance statements|severe-but-plausible scenarios
notes: Expand post CPS 230 effective date 2025-07-01

control_description: Named accountable persons for AI-supported critical operations aligned with Banking Executive Accountability Regime (BEAR)/FAR where applicable.
mandatory_records: FAR accountability statement including AI|responsible-person register|board attestation
notes: FAR replaced BEAR from 2024-03 — align accountable-person assignments accordingly

control_description: AI-related operational incidents must be reported to APRA under CPS 230 notification obligations; entities must track AI incidents in their register.
mandatory_records: AI incident register|APRA notification records|root cause analysis
notes: Notification timeline per CPS 230 — verify exact hours

control_description: Entities must test and monitor AI systems as part of operational risk management including scenario analysis and control-effectiveness testing.
mandatory_records: AI scenario analyses|control-effectiveness tests|monitoring metrics
notes: Expand — APRA supervisory engagements 2025-26 indicate deeper testing expectations

framework_name: ASIC AI Regulatory Expectations (REP 798 + s912A + 2026 priorities)
control_name: Corporations Act efficient honest fairly duty
control_description: AFSL holders must provide financial services efficiently honestly and fairly under s912A of the Corporations Act 2001; AI-mediated actions remain bound by this duty.
mandatory_records: AFSL compliance plan covering AI|breach reporting under RG 78|conduct monitoring records
notes: Evergreen obligation — ASIC explicitly reminds firms AI does not dilute it

framework_name: ASIC AI Regulatory Expectations (REP 798 + s912A + 2026 priorities)
control_name: Governance framework must match AI adoption pace
control_description: Entities must ensure their governance framework keeps pace with AI adoption; ASIC has warned of a governance gap where adoption has outpaced controls.
mandatory_records: AI governance framework|AI inventory vs control map|governance-to-adoption gap analysis
notes: Core finding of REP 798 — expand

framework_name: ASIC AI Regulatory Expectations (REP 798 + s912A + 2026 priorities)
control_description: ASIC 2026 enforcement priorities target advanced technology harming consumers including automated decisions and agentic AI.
mandatory_records: consumer impact assessments|complaints logs tagged for AI|harm scenarios reviewed
notes: Verify exact enforcement priority wording against ASIC 2026 Corporate Plan

framework_name: ASIC AI Regulatory Expectations (REP 798 + s912A + 2026 priorities)
control_description: Multi-step autonomous agent systems are a specific ASIC 2026 enforcement priority; firms using agentic AI must demonstrate controls proportionate to the increased autonomy risk.
mandatory_records: agentic-AI specific risk assessment|autonomy-level classification|circuit-breaker controls
notes: Directly relevant to multi-agent debate architecture — DATAPAI stock vertical

framework_name: ASIC AI Regulatory Expectations (REP 798 + s912A + 2026 priorities)
control_description: Market participants using AI in trading execution or surveillance remain subject to market integrity rules and must demonstrate AI does not create disorderly markets.
mandatory_records: market integrity procedures covering AI|surveillance logs|anomaly reports
source_section: Market Integrity Rules (Competition in Exchange Markets) 2011
notes: Relevant if broker / exchange / market-maker clients use the product

framework_name: ASIC AI Regulatory Expectations (REP 798 + s912A + 2026 priorities)
control_description: Where AI is used in consumer-facing financial services firms must disclose its use consistent with ASIC disclosure guidance and the Design and Distribution Obligations (DDO).
mandatory_records: AI disclosure content|TMD documentation referencing AI|disclosure consistency review
notes: Expand — link to AU 6 P4 Transparency

framework_name: NSW AI Assessment Framework (AIAF) — Circular DCS-2024-04
framework_publisher: NSW Government Department of Customer Service / Digital NSW
control_name: Mandatory application of AIAF to all NSW Government AI use
control_description: All NSW Government use of AI — including any AI system or component across its full lifecycle whether or not part of a formal project — must apply the NSW AI Assessment Framework per Circular DCS-2024-04.
mandatory_records: AIAF workbook for each AI use case|AI register entry|sign-off audit record
notes: Verify Circular number against current NSW Digital policy

framework_name: NSW AI Assessment Framework (AIAF) — Circular DCS-2024-04
framework_publisher: NSW Government Department of Customer Service / Digital NSW
control_description: Confirm the right expertise is involved and capture key use-case information (description responsible officer date completed) — Section 1 of the AIAF workbook.
mandatory_records: use-case description|responsible officer assignment|completion date record

framework_name: NSW AI Assessment Framework (AIAF) — Circular DCS-2024-04
framework_publisher: NSW Government Department of Customer Service / Digital NSW
control_description: Answer the 16 mandatory assessment questions; responses generate the overall risk level and list required assurance activities including PIA HRIA and Cybersecurity review.
mandatory_records: completed 16-question assessment|generated risk level|PIA if triggered|HRIA if triggered|cybersecurity review if triggered

framework_name: NSW AI Assessment Framework (AIAF) — Circular DCS-2024-04
framework_publisher: NSW Government Department of Customer Service / Digital NSW
control_name: Deep dive on additional focus areas (Section 3 — optional but recommended)
control_description: Work through the optional focus questions to identify further risks and link to good-practice controls — Section 3 of the AIAF.
mandatory_records: deep-dive focus-question responses|control mapping
notes: Optional but recommended — Digital NSW strongly encourages

framework_name: NSW AI Assessment Framework (AIAF) — Circular DCS-2024-04
framework_publisher: NSW Government Department of Customer Service / Digital NSW
control_description: Record sign-off in the Audit Record save the file in the agency's records management system and register High- or Critical-risk use cases in the agency's AI register.
mandatory_records: audit record|RMS-stored assessment|agency AI register entry

framework_name: NSW AI Assessment Framework (AIAF) — Circular DCS-2024-04
framework_publisher: NSW Government Department of Customer Service / Digital NSW
control_name: NSW AI Review Committee (AIRC) review for High/Critical use cases
control_description: High- or Critical-risk AI use cases identified by the AIAF must be submitted to the AI Secretariat which coordinates NSW AI Review Committee (AIRC) review.
mandatory_records: AIRC submission|AIRC review outcome|conditions imposed by AIRC

framework_name: NSW AI Assessment Framework (AIAF) — Circular DCS-2024-04
framework_publisher: NSW Government Department of Customer Service / Digital NSW
control_name: Privacy Impact Assessment for AI use cases
control_description: Where the 16-question AIAF assessment triggers it a Privacy Impact Assessment must be completed against NSW Privacy and Personal Information Protection Act 1998.
mandatory_records: completed Privacy Impact Assessment|PIPP Act 1998 alignment evidence|data flow diagram

framework_name: NSW AI Assessment Framework (AIAF) — Circular DCS-2024-04
framework_publisher: NSW Government Department of Customer Service / Digital NSW
control_name: Human Rights Impact Assessment for AI use cases
control_description: Where the 16-question AIAF assessment triggers it a Human Rights Impact Assessment must be completed covering affected groups and potential discrimination or exclusion.
mandatory_records: completed Human Rights Impact Assessment|affected-group analysis|mitigation plan

control_description: Entities must maintain a register of material service providers by 1 October 2025 (CPS 230 para 59); AI model vendors and AI-hosting cloud providers typically qualify as material.
mandatory_records: material service provider register|AI provider materiality assessment|register update cadence
notes: Specific 1 Oct 2025 deadline — material-arrangement notification

control_description: Entities must report breaches of tolerances for critical operations to APRA (CPS 230 para 42); AI outages in critical ops are in scope.
mandatory_records: critical operations tolerance statements|tolerance breach register|APRA notifications per para 42

control_description: Operational risk incidents affecting financial or critical operations must be reported to APRA per CPS 230 para 33 — AI-caused incidents (model failure wrong output sustained outage) are in scope.
mandatory_records: incident notifications to APRA|AI-cause tagging|root cause analysis

control_name: Board oversight of operational risk (including AI)
control_description: The board is responsible for the operational risk management framework under CPS 230 including AI-related operational risks; evidence of board-level discussion and approval is expected.
mandatory_records: board papers covering AI risk|board risk committee minutes|annual board attestation

control_name: Due diligence on AI service providers
control_description: Entities must conduct and document due diligence on AI service providers before engagement — including model governance data-handling practices and exit feasibility.
mandatory_records: pre-engagement due diligence reports|provider risk assessments|annual reassessment records

control_name: Exit and continuity plans for AI providers
control_description: Entities must maintain exit and continuity plans for material AI service providers including data-extraction rights and model-substitution capability.
mandatory_records: exit plans per material AI provider|continuity test results|contract termination clauses

framework_name: ASIC AI Regulatory Expectations (REP 798 + s912A + 2026 priorities)
control_description: AI-related compliance failures by AFSL holders fall within the reportable situations regime and must be reported to ASIC per Regulatory Guide 78 within the statutory timeframe.
mandatory_records: RG 78 breach register|AI-root-cause classification|90-day timeline tracking
notes: REP 798 governance gap increases RG 78 reporting frequency

framework_name: ASIC AI Regulatory Expectations (REP 798 + s912A + 2026 priorities)
control_name: Design and Distribution Obligations and Target Market Determination
control_description: Financial products involving AI-driven recommendations or personalisation remain subject to DDO including TMD review requirements under Regulatory Guide 274.
mandatory_records: Target Market Determination documents referencing AI|TMD review cadence|product-distribution records
notes: Relevant for AI-assisted personal advice or personalised PDS

framework_name: ASIC AI Regulatory Expectations (REP 798 + s912A + 2026 priorities)
control_description: REP 798 reviewed 23 AFS and credit licensees — finding 60% intend to ramp AI and governance arrangements are slow to change; firms must evidence that governance keeps pace with AI-adoption plans.
mandatory_records: AI-adoption roadmap vs governance-update schedule|board review of gap|closure-plan minutes
notes: Headline REP 798 number — 60% licensees ramping AI

framework_name: ASIC AI Regulatory Expectations (REP 798 + s912A + 2026 priorities)
control_description: REP 798 identified inadequate due diligence on third-party AI vendors; licensees must evidence vendor governance including model provenance and training-data attestations.
mandatory_records: vendor due-diligence files|model-provenance attestations|training-data source declarations
